headline: Tech's worst week in a year - AI momentum breaks
detail: The broad AI trade unraveled this week, hitting exactly the high-beta, momentum-heavy names you own hardest. AVGO and PLTR are both on SELL status for a reason - this isn't just noise. Until there's a clear catalyst to reverse sentiment, your concentrated tech book stays vulnerable.

headline: U.S. strikes Iran after Strait of Hormuz violation
detail: A confirmed retaliatory military strike is pulling oil higher in after-hours trading. Geopolitical flare-ups like this push investors toward safety and away from risk assets - bad timing for a tech-heavy, margin-leveraged book. Watch how futures open tomorrow.

headline: Micron sinks 6%, chips under pressure
detail: Micron's ugly week is a direct signal for your chip exposure - AVGO and SMH both live in this neighborhood. SMH is still up 10% for you and on HOLD, but a rolling chip selloff could test that cushion fast. Keep an eye on where SMH trades relative to its $540 stop.

headline: Oracle worst week since dot-com bust on AI financing fears
detail: Oracle's collapse on AI financing concerns suggests the market is starting to question whether AI spending translates to profits - the same narrative risk that's weighing on AVGO and PLTR. If that skepticism spreads, high-multiple AI names get hit hardest, and that's your book.
